definition: Claude Code's per-project MCP server configuration.
Where subagent definitions live. One markdown file per subagent.
Stagehand's LLM-driven action mode: describe what you want in natural language, model picks selector and action.
A system where the LLM dynamically directs its own process and tool usage. The model holds the script.
Terminal-driven coding agent with diff-based edit workflow.
Techniques to make automated browser sessions look like real users.
Web scraping platform Potter uses as a fallback to Proxycurl.
TypeScript schema library with runtime and compile-time type alignment.
Automatic speech recognition. Audio in, text out. Whisper is the default; many hosted variants exist.
Automatic speech recognition. Audio in, text out. Whisper, Deepgram, AssemblyAI.
Skip re-researching an attendee already briefed in the past 30 days. Use cached brief if recent.
Persistent record of every tool call, decision, and approval the harness handled.
Persistent, structured record of every agent decision and tool call.
A single LLM call enriched with retrieval, tools, and memory. The atomic unit every pattern in this module is built from.
Long-running agent that opens its own pull request.
The implicit dial in every pattern: how many decisions you hand to the model in exchange for adaptability. Higher autonomy means more flexibility and less predictability.
An agent run on a schedule or in response to events, no user blocking.
The ability for the user to interrupt the agent mid-speech.
An API tier that processes requests asynchronously at lower cost and longer latency. Roughly half-price on Anthropic and OpenAI.
API tier that processes requests asynchronously at lower cost.
Async tier with 50% cost discount and 24-hour SLA.
Potter's background runtime for scheduled enrichment jobs.
Eval that scores whether the agent took the right actions, not just whether the final answer was right.
Outbound voice agent platform, often used for high-volume sales and reminders.
Hosted eval platform for LLM apps.
The structured one-page summary per qualified company, fed to outbound or sales.
Structured one-page document delivered to the user before the meeting.
Long-form artifact (using Remotion) attached to the Slack message for top hits.
The one Potter tool that calls an LLM internally, used for dynamic UI navigation.
Managed cloud browser runtime for agents. Sessions, contexts, proxies, anti-detection.
The decision to handcraft a harness (build) vs use a managed platform (buy).
"Bring your own key." A pattern where end users supply their own API credentials instead of going through a vendor's billing.
The cache_control field added to a content block to mark it cacheable.
How long a cached prefix remains warm. Anthropic's ephemeral cache is roughly 5 minutes.
Repeated prompt prefixes that are billed at a fraction of standard input rate.
Push notification from a calendar service (Calendly, Google Calendar) on new event creation.
Rolling out a change to a small fraction of traffic before full rollout.
Normalized form of a company URL (lowercase, no www, trailing slash stripped) used as a stable identifier.
A check before a tool runs, classifying it as auto-allowed, confirm-required, or denied.
The MCP handshake where client and server agree on which features are supported.
A locked document defining what a major version of a tool layer must do, with concrete acceptance criteria.
Reasoning trace before the answer. Without tools, prone to fabrication. With tools, becomes ReAct.
Splitting documents into smaller pieces for embedding and retrieval.
Anthropic's first-party SDK (TypeScript and Python) for building agents on top of the Claude API with first-party loop, tools, memory, and hooks.
Anthropic's terminal-based coding agent harness. Module 8 covers the extension surface.
Command-line tool layered on top of the SDK that adds UI, file access, permissions, hooks, subagents.
Common failure where the model wraps JSON in markdown code fences, breaking parsers.
OpenAI's coding agent CLI. Interactive mode plus a non-interactive `codex exec` entry point used for review-shaped runs and CI integrations.
An agent specialized for code: file system as state, tests as eval, git as reversibility.
Unbounded growth of conversation context that inflates per-call cost.
The harness's strategy for keeping conversation context under the model's window.
Replacing earlier conversation turns with a summary to free working memory.
Maximum tokens (input + output) the model can attend to in a single call. 1M on Opus 4.7 and Sonnet 4.6; 200K on Haiku 4.5.
A specific recent fact about the attendee or company worth mentioning in the meeting opening.
Scheduling pattern that runs work at fixed intervals (every minute, hour, day).
Editor with built-in agent mode and rules files.
A harness you wrote yourself, usually 50 to 500 lines, scoped to one job.
Production inputs diverging from the golden dataset over time.
Where failed events land after max retries.
The rule for picking the next token from the model's output probability distribution.
Preventing the same company from surfacing in two consecutive weekly digests.
Marking a tool as discouraged in its description so the model avoids picking it.
The principle that tool descriptions function as prompts, not docs, because the model reads them to decide.
Cognition's autonomous coding agent that ships GitHub PRs.
Agent edits expressed as diffs to the working tree, applied atomically.
Injection from the user typing into the agent.
Domain authentication records that help receiving mail servers trust that a sender is legitimate.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: Models or APIs designed for PDFs, docx, structured documents, with layout and table awareness.
Mandatory --dry-run mode that logs what would happen without creating Gmail drafts.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: Pattern where two parties (e.g. agent and human) must approve a privileged action.
Workflow execution that survives crashes via persisted state. Inngest, Trigger.dev, Temporal.
A vector representation of text used for semantic similarity.
A schema constraint that limits a field to a closed list of values.
Brief delivered to a Slack DM (private) and optionally expiring 24h after the meeting.
Records of past sessions, persisted, retrievable by user or time.
Surfacing a stuck job to a human after retries are exhausted.
Fraction of runs that surface to a human reviewer. A signal of agent quality.
Built-in tooling for golden tests, LLM-as-judge, regression. Module 16 deep-dives.
Layered testing strategy: unit, behavioral, golden, red-team, production sampling.
Pattern: a generator LLM produces output, an evaluator LLM critiques it, the generator revises. Loops until the evaluator passes or a cap is hit.
Retry policy where delay doubles each attempt, capped.
Anthropic's reasoning mode. You opt in via a thinking budget; response includes thinking content blocks alongside text and tool_use.
Meeting participant whose email domain is not in the user's internal-domain list.
The pattern of pulling specific fields out of unstructured text into a structured object.
Including worked examples in the prompt. Strong tool for format and edge-case control. Easy to overuse.
Updating model weights on your data. Rarely beats RAG for agents.
Web crawl provider Potter uses for general web research.
A test or automation that passes sometimes and fails sometimes for non-deterministic reasons.
API parameter that requires the model to call a specific tool, used for extraction.
An agent run with a user waiting on the response.
A single trip of input through every layer of the network, producing output logits.
Both sides can talk simultaneously. Phone-call mode.
The data API or scraper providing this week's raise list. Crunchbase, Apify, NewsAPI, RSS.
EU regulation governing personal data processing. Affects agent design when EU users are in scope.
The dominant quantization format for local inference.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Per-user and per-project API limits that require pacing and retry behavior when creating drafts in batches.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: An email saved in the user's Gmail Drafts folder through the Gmail API, not sent automatically.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: Fixed set of inputs with known expected outputs used as the eval baseline.
Google Cloud's enterprise agent platform built on Vertex AI.
Pick the highest-probability token at every step. Deterministic, often repetitive on long generations.
Always pick the highest-probability next token. The limit of temperature 0. Most stable but most repetitive.
Anthropic's three tiers, ordered cheap to capable, used in routing decisions.
One side talks at a time. Walkie-talkie mode.
Increase in factual error rate over time, often after a model version change.
The program that runs the agentic loop, executes tools, and provides the user interface. Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, your custom script.
The CLI or app that runs the agent loop. Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, your custom script.
The program that runs the agent loop, manages tools, exposes UI.
Hosted LLM observability and gateway service.
A user-defined callback the harness invokes at lifecycle events.
Shell command Claude Code runs at a lifecycle event (e.g. before a tool call).
Agent state (sessions, memory) persisted on the vendor's servers rather than yours.
The raw endpoint a provider exposes. Lowest layer; you pay for everything above it.
The gravitational center of the open-weights ecosystem. Hub, Spaces, Datasets, Endpoints.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Combining keyword (BM25) and vector similarity for retrieval.
Structured criteria (industry, stage, geography, headcount) used to qualify leads before deep research.
Filter so narrow it rejects warm leads. Detected via eval drift.
A stable identifier preventing duplicate work when a job runs twice.
Stable key, usually derived from normalized email, preventing the same lead from being processed twice.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: A model producing an image from a text prompt or a reference image. gpt-image-2, Flux, Stable Diffusion.
Workflow where a generated UI mockup is handed to a coding agent that produces matching JSX/CSS.
Injection from data the agent reads (emails, PDFs, web pages, tool results).
The runtime act of using a trained model. Each call is one forward pass through frozen weights.
A company or piece of software that runs inference for you. Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Together, vLLM.
Inngest's cron primitive for durable scheduled jobs.
Image generation that edits a region of an existing image while keeping the rest. Sister to image-out from scratch.
Where instructions sit in the prompt affects how strongly the model attends to them. Top and bottom positions carry more weight than the middle.
Email domain belonging to the user's own organization. Used to filter attendees.
The boolean field on tool_result that signals failure to the model.
An attack that tries to override the agent's safety constraints.
Randomization added to retry delays so clients do not sync up.
An API setting that requires the response to be a valid JSON object.
How often the LLM judge and a human reviewer agree on the same response.
Open-source LLM observability platform.
The cost of switching between API, SDK, CLI, MCP layers later in the project.
Meta's open-weights model line. Llama 3.x, Llama 4.x, etc.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: The C++ inference engine under most local-model tools.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Using a model to score another model's output against a rubric.
A phrase that makes text read machine-written, such as "just wanted to" or "touching base".
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: GUI app for running open-weights models locally.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Authentication redirect that breaks deterministic flows. Often requires manual handoff.
Pre-softmax raw scores the model produces for each vocabulary token. Logits feed into the decoding rule.
Low-Rank Adaptation. Fine-tune a small subset of weights, cheap and fast.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: A vendor offering that runs your agent loop, persists state, ships deployment.
API parameter capping output length. Default values are usually too high for cost.
Model Context Protocol. The standard for exposing tools to LLM harnesses.
Model Context Protocol. Standard for exposing tools to LLM harnesses.
The act of connecting an MCP server to a harness so its tools become available.
Reusable prompt templates exposed by an MCP server, alongside tools.
Read-only data sources exposed by an MCP server, alongside tools.
The Potter runtime exposing tools via the Model Context Protocol.
The pre-meeting research a salesperson does manually; this agent automates it.
Delivering the brief 30 min before meeting in the meeting's local time, not the user's.
Anthropic's first-party memory primitive: file-system-style commands (view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename) over a /memories/ directory; storage is client-side.
Picking a different model per turn or per use case based on cost or capability needs.
A model that accepts or produces more than one modality (text, image, audio, video).
A leading word identifying a tool's family (e.g. potter_research_*).
Tool names beginning with the layer's identifier (potter_*) to disambiguate at scale.
Codename for a Google image generation model in the Gemini family.
Wrapper around llama.cpp for easy local model serving. Default for most local inference.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Model files released for download, often with a permissive license. Different from "open source" which implies training code too.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: OpenAI's hosted offering for building agents via SDK or visual graph editor.
Open standard for tracing, metrics, logs.
Pattern: an orchestrator LLM plans subtasks at runtime, dispatches each to a worker LLM, and synthesizes the worker outputs.
Agent produces all emails but does not create Gmail drafts until a human approves.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: Checking model output against an expected shape before passing it to UI, shell, or DB.
Agent that works alongside a developer in real-time, suggesting and applying edits.
A single model response containing multiple tool_use blocks the harness should run concurrently.
Running multiple LLM calls concurrently and combining outputs. Two flavors: sectioning (different chunks) and voting (same prompt, multiple runs).
Truncated JSON output, usually from hitting max_tokens mid-generation.
Provider charges per million tokens, separated into input and output.
Tools auto-approved for execution without prompting the user.
The check the harness runs before executing a tool, asking the user if needed.
List of pre-approved tool calls the harness can run without asking.
The role assigned in the system prompt. Frames register, vocabulary, and judgement defaults. Bad for hard rules; good for tone.
Voice drifts across a batch as prompt context changes. Mitigated by system prompt caching and stable few-shots.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: Email generation tuned to a specific user's voice via system prompt and few-shot examples.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: Personally identifiable information. Detect, redact, never log raw.
Mode where the agent writes a plan but does not execute changes.
Asking the model to describe its plan before issuing tool calls.
Microsoft's browser automation library. Substrate for most agent browser tools.
A bundle of skills, hooks, and tools that extend a harness.
Bundle of skills, hooks, slash commands, and tools distributed as a directory.
Hook event firing after a tool call returns. Receives the result.
Validation pass before any external write to catch bad subject lines, banned phrases, or body shape errors.
moduleTitle: GTM agent: personalized cold email drafter

definition: The phase where input tokens are processed in a batch before generation starts. Dominates TTFT on long inputs.
Hook event firing before a tool call executes. Can deny.
Running judge prompts against a fraction of real traffic for ongoing eval.
Optimization that lets the API cache the prefix of repeated requests, charging much less for cached portions.
Workflow pattern: decompose a task into ordered LLM calls, each one a smaller, more focused job than the original.
Adversarial input embedded in a tool result, document, or message that tries to override the system prompt. Treat tool output as data, not instructions. Module 12 covers defenses.
An attack that smuggles instructions into the model's context through user input or fetched data.
Open-source eval framework for prompts and agents.
Routing a request to a secondary provider when the primary fails or rate-limits.
The cost of switching the underlying model provider once you have built on a platform.
LinkedIn data provider used as Potter's primary research source.
Python equivalent of Zod, popular in agent frameworks.
LoRA on a quantized base model. Even cheaper.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Reducing model precision (fp16 to int8 to int4) to fit smaller hardware, with quality trade-off.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Event store decoupling producers and consumers; events trigger agent runs.
Alibaba's open-weights model line. Strong on code and multilingual.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Retrieval-Augmented Generation. The pattern of retrieving relevant documents and injecting them into the prompt.
Reasoning and acting interleaved. Pattern from Yao et al. (arXiv 2022, ICLR 2023): Thought, Action, Observation, repeat. Modern Claude tool-use is ReAct natively.
OpenAI's audio-in audio-out streaming API with function calling. GA since August 2025; WebRTC for browser/mobile, WebSocket for server-to-server.
Models that emit hidden chain-of-thought before the user-visible answer. Claude extended thinking, OpenAI o-series, Gemini Thinking.
Adversarial inputs designed to trigger failures (injection, jailbreak, edge cases).
Re-running the eval suite against a new model version to catch drift.
1-5 ranking of how well a company matches the ICP, with one-sentence rationale.
Re-running a failed agent run from persisted state to debug.
A second-pass scoring of retrieved candidates by a (often smaller) model.
A proxy whose IP belongs to a real ISP, used to evade anti-bot blocks.
API parameter that takes a schema and constrains the response to match.
Managed voice agent platform, phone-first focus.
Workflow pattern: classify the input and dispatch to one of N specialized downstream paths.
Structured scoring criteria for the judge prompt.
Correlation identifier shared by all events from a single agent run.
Isolation environment for executing tool code. Container per call, network deny by default.
The JSON Schema describing a tool's input arguments and types.
The contract describing the shape and types of a structured response.
Checking that a parsed object matches the expected shape and types.
Typed library wrapping the HTTP API in your language of choice. Anthropic SDK in TypeScript and Python.
Parallelization variant: split the input into independent chunks, run each in parallel, combine the results.
A query (CSS, XPath, role+text) identifying which element to interact with.
Specific case where a selector works once and fails the next due to dynamic class names or A/B tests.
A knowledge base of facts or documents the agent retrieves from on demand.
Pre-filtering the tool catalog by relevance to the current query before showing the model.
Override mechanism (global, project, local) for harness configuration.
Claude Code's configuration file. Layered: global, user, project, project-local.
Sending a copy of production traffic to a new version without serving its responses.
Prompt-templated capability defined by a markdown file with frontmatter. The model decides when to invoke it.
User-typed shortcut that expands to a templated prompt.
Service Level Objective. The target reliability metric (e.g. 99% of requests under 2s).
A unit of work with start/end timestamps, used for distributed tracing.
The practice of writing the spec first, locking acceptance, then implementing.
Workflow where the spec is written first and the agent implements against it.
Per-user or per-feature limit that prevents runaway cost.
Server-Sent Events. The streaming format the API uses to deliver tokens incrementally.
MCP over Server-Sent Events. Used by older hosted MCP servers.
Browser automation library combining Playwright primitives with LLM-driven act() mode.
How Potter wraps Stagehand for browser automation inside potter_browser_act.
An HTTP API where the server does not persist any state about clients between requests. Anthropic's /v1/messages is stateless.
MCP over stdin and stdout pipes. Used by local server processes.
A unit of durable execution; runs once and persists its result for replay.
Field on every API response telling you why generation stopped. Drives the agentic loop's control flow.
A newer MCP transport using HTTP with chunked responses. The default for new hosted servers.
An error response shaped as JSON the model can introspect, not a free-text string.
A log line with explicit fields (run_id, tool_name, status), not a free-text message.
A model response constrained to a parseable shape (JSON object, typed fields).
A child agent loop launched by the parent. Has its own context window and tools, returns a single result, does not see parent context.
A scoped agent the main loop spawns to do a sub-task with its own context.
A scoped agent the main loop spawns with its own context, tool set, and system prompt.
Open-ended question the agent recommends asking, tied to the attendee's recent activity.
Public benchmark for coding agents using real GitHub issues.
The durable instruction layer sent on every turn of the loop. Lives in the top-level system field, separate from the messages array.
US regulation governing automated calls and texts. Affects outbound voice agents.
Sampling parameter. 0 means deterministic-ish, higher means more variety in output.
Sampling parameter that scales the next-token probability distribution. Default 1.0, range 0.0 to 1.0. Disabled on Claude Opus 4.7 (omit the parameter; steer behavior via prompting).
Content block type holding reasoning the model did before answering. Billed and counted toward latency; harness chooses whether to render.
Shipping the same core under three deployment wrappers (MCP, batch, CLI) for different consumption shapes.
Tokens per second during decoding. Independent of how long the input is.
Sub-word fragment the model operates on. Roughly four characters of English per token.
Predicted monthly token consumption from product metrics.
The complete set of tools available to an agent in a session.
The principle that tool descriptions are read by the model as part of the prompt and should be written with the same care as instructions, including disambiguating examples.
Per-tool execution policy (auto, confirm, deny) by user or context.
The abstraction level of a browser tool (low-level click_x_y vs high-level purchase_product).
Where the harness keeps the available tools the model can invoke.
Maintaining v1 and v2 of a tool so existing prompts and new prompts can both work.
Content blocks. tool_use is the model asking to run a function; tool_result is your response with the output.
Nucleus sampling. Only consider tokens whose cumulative probability reaches p, then sample from that nucleus. Anthropic recommends it only for advanced cases.
Sampling rule that only considers the k highest-probability tokens. Hard cutoff regardless of the distribution shape.
Sampling rule that only considers tokens whose cumulative probability reaches p. Adapts to the shape of the distribution.
Marker on input data indicating its source's trust level (trusted, untrusted, hostile).
Time to first token. The wall time between request submission and the first generated token arriving.
Time to first audible token. The wall time between user finishing and agent's first sound.
Text-to-speech. Text in, audio out. ElevenLabs, OpenAI TTS, many open-source options.
Text-to-speech. Text in, audio out. ElevenLabs, OpenAI TTS, Cartesia.
Hook event firing when the user submits a prompt. Receives the prompt, can mutate or block.
Voice activity detection. Determines when the user is speaking versus silent.
Managed voice agent platform orchestrating ASR + LLM + TTS via API.
A database optimized for similarity search over embeddings.
Provider-agnostic TypeScript SDK with streaming-first React helpers.
A model accepting images as input and returning text. The most common multimodal pattern in production.
A graph-style UI for composing agents, nodes connected by edges.
Sending screenshots back to the model so it can "see" the current page state.
High-throughput inference server for hosted serving.
moduleTitle: Open source models and Hugging Face

definition: Parallelization variant: run the same prompt N times to get diverse outputs, then aggregate (majority vote, average, ensemble). The aggregation step reduces variance. Does not help with structural blind spots.
The Monday Slack message containing the ranked list of qualified raises.
A system where LLMs and tools are orchestrated through predefined code paths. Your code holds the script.
The current conversation's context. Lives in the messages array of one session.
The thin profit margin LLM-wrapper startups operate on.
No examples in the prompt, only instructions. The default for most agent prompts on capable models.
TypeScript schema library used to validate parsed objects on the client side.
